comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: LEARNED: A bash check named 'warn only' that calls exit 1 on a missing optional tool silently becomes a release blocker. Use exit 0 or continue-on-error: true for truly non-blocking CI checks.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: knowledge.jsonl entries generated during a session can carry shell artifacts (" 2>&1") in content fields if the capture hook collects redirect noise. Always validate new entries with jq before committing.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: LEARNED: .claude/rules/ files are loaded globally by Claude Code — adding the same rule list to an individual SKILL.md creates a second source of truth that will drift. Cross-reference the rules file instead of duplicating.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: LEARNED: Dev convenience files (.claude/launch.json, etc.) committed inside a feature PR inflate review surface and couple unrelated concerns. Gitignore local-only artifacts or commit them separately on main.

title: Rewrite .claude/skills/ paths in platform converters instead of runtime fallback chain
description:
## Issue

lavra-work-ralph.md, lavra-work-teams.md, and lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md currently use a runtime fallback chain to locate the subagent prompt template across platforms:

```bash
_TMPL="lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md"
AGENT_TEMPLATE=$(cat ".claude/skills/$_TMPL" 2>/dev/null || cat ".opencode/skills/$_TMPL" 2>/dev/null || ...)
```

This is the wrong layer. Platform path differences are a conversion-time concern, not a runtime concern. The converters already handle platform-specific transformations (model IDs, command formats, etc.).

## What

1. Revert ralph.md, teams.md, and lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md to a single clean canonical path:
   ```bash
   AGENT_TEMPLATE=$(cat ".claude/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md")
   ```

2. Add a content rewrite step in each platform converter's convertCommands() (and convertSkills()) function that substitutes `.claude/skills/` with the platform-correct path:
   - convert-opencode.ts: `.claude/skills/` → `.opencode/skills/`
   - convert-gemini.ts: `.claude/skills/` → `skills/`
   - Cortex converter (if exists): `.claude/skills/` → `.cortex/skills/`

   Global install paths (e.g. ~/.config/opencode) are not needed — at runtime the agent is always in the project directory, and the installed skill is always at the project-local path.

3. Revert the installer changes to OpenCode and Gemini from lavra-ceh.7 (the cp -r fix for references/ is still correct and should stay — only remove the fallback chain logic from the command/skill markdown files).

## Why

- Runtime fallback chains are fragile and hide misconfiguration
- Converters are the established pattern for platform differences in this codebase
- Each platform gets a single correct hardcoded path — no silent fallthrough
- Consistent with how model tiers, $ARGUMENTS, and command formats are already handled

## Validation
- [ ] lavra-work-ralph.md (Claude source) contains only `.claude/skills/` path
- [ ] opencode/commands/lavra-work-ralph.md contains `.opencode/skills/` path after conversion
- [ ] gemini/commands/lavra-work-ralph.md contains `skills/` path after conversion
- [ ] Same substitution applies to lavra-work-teams.md and lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md
- [ ] Conversion scripts produce correct output on re-run
- [ ] No runtime fallback chain remains in any of the three files

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: path rewriting belongs in converters not runtime — each platform's generated files get a single correct hardcoded path baked in at install time. Pattern: add .replace() in both convertCommands() and convertSkills() of each converter script.

title: Fix hardcoded .claude/skills path in subagent template cat command
description:
## Issue

lavra-work-ralph.md and lavra-work-teams.md now reference:

```bash
AGENT_TEMPLATE=$(cat .claude/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md)
```

This path is Claude Code-specific. Both commands exist on OpenCode, Gemini, and Cortex — none of which install to .claude/skills/. The cat will fail silently, producing an empty template.

Platform skill paths:
- Claude Code: .claude/skills/
- OpenCode: .opencode/skills/ (or skills/ for global)
- Gemini: skills/ (project root)
- Cortex: .cortex/skills/ (or skills/ for global)

## What

Two changes needed:

1. Replace the hardcoded path in lavra-work-ralph.md and lavra-work-teams.md with a fallback chain:
```bash
AGENT_TEMPLATE=$(cat .claude/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md 2>/dev/null || cat .opencode/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md 2>/dev/null || cat skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md 2>/dev/null || cat .cortex/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md 2>/dev/null)
```

2. Update each platform installer to copy the references/ subdirectory alongside the skill:
- installers/install-opencode.sh: copy references/ when installing lavra-work-multi skill
- installers/install-gemini.sh: same
- installers/install-cortex.sh: same
- The Claude installer (install.sh) already copies the full skills/ tree so references/ is included automatically

Also update lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md Phase M7 cat command with the same fallback chain (it is Claude Code-only in practice since Task() is Claude-specific, but defense-in-depth).

## Validation
- [ ] Fallback chain resolves correctly on each platform (cat first match wins)
- [ ] references/subagent-prompt.md is present in installed skill dirs for all platforms
- [ ] lavra-work-ralph and lavra-work-teams work on OpenCode/Gemini/Cortex

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: used _TMPL variable + || fallback chain covering all platform paths (project-local .claude/.opencode/.cortex/skills/, Gemini project-root skills/, global ~/.config/opencode and ~/.snowflake/cortex) — first successful cat wins. Updated OpenCode and Gemini installers to copy references/ subdirectory alongside SKILL.md. Cortex already uses cp -r.

title: Think Before Planning gate only fires on no-brainstorm path in lavra-plan
description:
## Issue

The 'Think Before Planning' block added in lavra-plan.md (lines 116-124) is nested inside Step 0d — 'No Brainstorm — Run Idea Refinement'. This means the assumption-surfacing gate does NOT fire when a brainstorm bead IS found (the Brainstorm Detected path, Step 0a-0c).

The discipline of stating interpretation, listing assumptions, and asking ONE focused question is universally valuable regardless of whether input came from a brainstorm bead or a raw description.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — a scoping error, not a YAGNI issue. The Karpathy 'Think Before Coding' principle is only partially applied to lavra-plan.

## Location
- plugins/lavra/commands/lavra-plan.md lines 116-124 (currently inside Step 0d only)

## Remediation
Move the Think Before Planning block one level up — before Step 0a (brainstorm detection) — so it fires for all input paths. Both the brainstorm path and the no-brainstorm path then require the agent to externalize its interpretation before proceeding.

Alternative: if the brainstorm path is considered already-disambiguated by definition (brainstorm beads have explicit intent), keep in Step 0d but add a note explaining why the brainstorm path is exempt.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] Think Before Planning fires when input is a brainstorm bead ID
- [ ] Think Before Planning fires when input is a raw feature description
- [ ] Clear requests still proceed without interruption (the 'say so and continue' bullet is preserved)

## Testing Steps
1. Read lavra-plan.md Step 0a-0d structure to verify the block position
2. Confirm the block appears before the brainstorm-detection branch, or that the exemption is documented

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: Moved Think Before Planning block to the top of Step 0 (before Step 0a), so it fires for all input paths — brainstorm and non-brainstorm alike. The block's core discipline (externalize interpretation, state assumptions, resolve ambiguity) applies equally before brainstorm detection begins, not just in the no-brainstorm fallback. Removed the duplicate block from Step 0d.

title: Remove duplicate success_criteria and guardrails from lavra-work.md router and skill files
description:
## Issue

After the lavra-work split, success_criteria and guardrails blocks appear in three places:
1. lavra-work.md (router) lines 77-142 — full blocks, ~65 lines
2. lavra-work-single/SKILL.md lines 458-510 — identical blocks
3. lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md lines 504-558 — identical blocks

The router never executes those phases — it immediately delegates to a skill. Any model reading the router then reads the skill and sees the criteria twice. This creates drift risk as the router copy will fall out of sync with the skills.

Additionally, both skill files contain a 'For Autonomous Retry or Persistent Workers' guardrail pointing to /lavra-work-ralph and /lavra-work-teams. This is only relevant at the user-facing router level — subagents running inside a skill would never invoke those commands.

Also: trailing success_criteria blocks at the end of both skill files recap criteria already enforced by the mandatory review-gate checklists mid-file (Phase 3 gate in single, Phase M8 gate in multi). These create a second source of truth.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — duplication creates drift risk and adds ~100 unnecessary lines across 3 files.

## Location
- lavra-work.md lines 77-142 (success_criteria + guardrails blocks to remove entirely)
- lavra-work-single/SKILL.md lines 458-470 (trailing success_criteria recap)
- lavra-work-single/SKILL.md lines 504-509 (For Autonomous Retry guardrail)
- lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md lines 504-513 (trailing success_criteria recap)
- lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md lines 552-557 (For Autonomous Retry guardrail)

## Remediation
- Delete success_criteria and guardrails from lavra-work.md entirely. Router ends at </process> (~line 75).
- Delete trailing success_criteria from both skill files — phase exit gates already enforce these.
- Delete 'For Autonomous Retry' guardrail from both skill files — irrelevant inside skills, keep only in router (but router is losing its guardrails block, so consider adding a brief cross-reference note in the router's objective block instead).

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] lavra-work.md ends at </process> with no trailing success_criteria or guardrails blocks
- [ ] lavra-work-single/SKILL.md has no trailing success_criteria block; review-gate checklist remains
- [ ] lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md has no trailing success_criteria block; Phase M8 exit gate remains
- [ ] 'For Autonomous Retry' guardrail removed from both skill files
- [ ] Total LOC reduction ~100 lines across the three files

## Testing Steps
1. Verify lavra-work.md line count is ~77 or less after change
2. Grep for duplicate success_criteria content across the three files — should find 0 duplicates

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: router (lavra-work.md) ends at </process> — no trailing success_criteria or guardrails. Skill files own those definitions. Cross-reference to ralph/teams removed from skill guardrails (irrelevant to subagents)

title: Sanitize RECALL_RESULTS and RELATED_BEADS before subagent prompt template insertion
description:
## Issue

In lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md Phase M6, `{RECALL_RESULTS}` and `{RELATED_BEADS}` are retrieved from user-contributed sources but are not sanitized or wrapped in untrusted XML before being inserted into the subagent prompt template.

`{RECALL_RESULTS}` comes from `.lavra/memory/recall.sh` which returns raw knowledge.jsonl content. When auto-recall.sh injects knowledge at session start, it calls `sanitize_untrusted_content` and wraps in `<untrusted-knowledge>` tags. Phase M6 does not.

`{RELATED_BEADS}` comes from `bd dep list {BEAD_ID} --json` relates_to entries — user-contributed bead content that is not piped through `extract-bead-context.sh` or sanitized independently.

Both land in the subagent template inside markdown blockquotes (`>`), which provide no security boundary to an LLM.

## Severity
P2 IMPORTANT — matches the exact threat model the untrusted XML wrapper pattern was designed to address. Collaborator with write access to beads DB can inject role-injection text that reaches subagents without wrapping.

## Location
- `plugins/lavra/skills/lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md` — Phase M6 recall and related-bead fetch steps
- `plugins/lavra/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md` lines 21-22, 31-33

## Remediation
In SKILL.md Phase M6, after calling `recall.sh`, pipe through `sanitize_untrusted_content` and wrap in `<untrusted-knowledge>` tags. Mirror auto-recall.sh lines 192-196.

For `{RELATED_BEADS}`: apply `sanitize_untrusted_content` + untrusted XML wrapping inline, or pass through `extract-bead-context.sh`.

Option B (defense-in-depth): also add hardcoded `<untrusted-knowledge>` wrapper tags in the template file itself around both placeholder slots.

## Validation Criteria
- [ ] `{RECALL_RESULTS}` in assembled subagent prompt is wrapped in `<untrusted-knowledge>` tags with do-not-follow directive
- [ ] `{RELATED_BEADS}` in assembled subagent prompt is wrapped in untrusted XML
- [ ] Both values pass through `sanitize_untrusted_content` before insertion
- [ ] Pattern matches auto-recall.sh wrapping approach

## Testing Steps
1. Read Phase M6 of lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md and verify sanitization is applied before storing RECALL_RESULTS
2. Verify the template placeholder sections in subagent-prompt.md have untrusted XML wrapper (defense-in-depth)

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: added untrusted-knowledge wrapping to RECALL_RESULTS and RELATED_BEADS in both Phase M6 instructions and subagent-prompt.md template — defense in depth: orchestrator wraps before fill, template wraps the slot regardless

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: all user-contributed content reaching subagent prompts must go through sanitize_untrusted_content + untrusted-knowledge XML wrapper — mirror auto-recall.sh lines 192-196 as the canonical pattern

title: Add Karpathy planning principles to lavra-plan
description:
Add Think Before Coding principle from Karpathy to lavra-plan's refinement step.

## What

In lavra-plan's refinement/clarification step (before research begins), add an explicit gate:
- State assumptions out loud before proceeding
- Surface ambiguities and ask one focused clarifying question if anything is unclear
- Present multiple interpretations when the request has more than one reasonable reading
- Only proceed when the feature description is unambiguous

This mirrors what Karpathy observed: models 'make wrong assumptions on your behalf and just run along with them without checking.'

## Where in lavra-plan

The refinement step (around line 116 — 'Gather signals for research decision'). Before locking in the feature description, the agent must externalize its interpretation and confirm or resolve any ambiguity.

## What NOT to add

- Simplicity First and Surgical Changes belong in lavra-work (implementation), not planning
- Goal-Driven Execution is already handled by goal-verifier

## Validation
- lavra-plan with an ambiguous input surfaces assumptions and asks a clarifying question before proceeding to research
- lavra-plan with a clear input proceeds without unnecessary interruption

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: Think Before Planning gate placed before research signals step — agent must externalize interpretation and assumptions before locking in feature description

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: Karpathy principle maps to lavra: Think Before Coding → lavra-plan clarification gate; Simplicity+Surgical → lavra-work Phase 2; Goal-Driven → goal-verifier (already exists)

title: Refactor lavra-plan and lavra-work and add Karpathy principles
description:
Epic tracking two refactors motivated by Karpathy's LLM coding principles.

## Why

Andrej Karpathy identified four LLM coding failure modes: silent assumptions, speculative complexity, untargeted edits, and vague goals. lavra-plan and lavra-work can enforce the first three directly. The fourth (goal-driven execution) is already handled by goal-verifier.

## Child beads

1. Split lavra-work into router + single/multi skills with lavra-worker template (lavra-drj)
2. Add Karpathy planning principles to lavra-plan (TBD)

## Karpathy principles mapping

- Think Before Coding (clarify assumptions, surface ambiguities) -> lavra-plan refinement step
- Simplicity First + Surgical Changes -> lavra-work-single Phase 2 + lavra-worker template
- Goal-Driven Execution -> already handled by goal-verifier, no change needed

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: LEARNED: {RECALL_RESULTS} and {RELATED_BEADS} in multi-bead subagent template are not sanitized or wrapped in untrusted XML — auto-recall.sh wraps via sanitize_untrusted_content but Phase M6 inline recall does not. Pattern: all user-contributed content reaching subagent prompts must go through sanitize_untrusted_content + untrusted-knowledge XML wrapper.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: when extracting content from a monolithic command into skills, check every injection point — sanitization that was implicit (e.g. auto-recall.sh) does not automatically transfer to the new path

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: LEARNED: success_criteria and guardrails blocks in a router command duplicate the skill files it delegates to — create drift risk. Router should end at the routing decision, not carry completion criteria it never enforces.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: Think Before Planning / clarification gates in planning commands should sit above branching logic, not inside one branch — otherwise some input paths silently bypass the gate

title: Split lavra-work into router + single/multi skills with lavra-worker template
description:
lavra-work.md is 1162 lines. Single-bead path loads ~650 lines of multi-bead machinery it never uses. Multi-bead subagent prompt is embedded in the command, making it hard to maintain or extend independently.

## What

Split lavra-work.md into:
- commands/lavra-work.md — Phase 0 routing only (~65 lines), delegates via Skill()
- skills/lavra-work-single/SKILL.md — Phases 1-5 (~440 lines, single-bead path)
- skills/lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md — Phases M1-M10 (~650 lines, multi-bead orchestrator)
- skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md — subagent prompt template (~130 lines), read via cat at M7, not an invocable skill

lavra-work-multi reads its own references/subagent-prompt.md at M7 via bash, fills placeholders, passes to Task().

## Why

- Token efficiency: single-bead drops ~650 lines from context every run
- Maintainability: subagent behavior editable without touching lavra-work.md
- Extensibility: karpathy-coding principles can be added to references/subagent-prompt.md and lavra-work-single/SKILL.md cleanly

## Karpathy integration

Add Simplicity First + Surgical Changes as a short block in:
- skills/lavra-work-single/SKILL.md Phase 2 entry
- skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md instructions

Think Before Coding belongs in lavra-plan (lavra-850), not here.
Goal-Driven Execution already handled by goal-verifier.

## Also update

- lavra-work-ralph.md line 168: update template source from 'Phase M7 of lavra-work' to 'cat .claude/skills/lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md'
- lavra-work-teams.md line 193: same update

## Validation
- Single-bead run: lavra-work-single/SKILL.md loads, lavra-work-multi/SKILL.md does NOT
- Multi-bead run: orchestrator reads references/subagent-prompt.md via cat and fills all 8 placeholders
- Subagent prompt contains Karpathy Simplicity + Surgical principles
- lavra-work-ralph and lavra-work-teams still function correctly
notes: lavra-work-ralph (line 168) and lavra-work-teams (line 193) both explicitly reference 'agent prompt template from /lavra-work Phase M7'. Both fill {PLACEHOLDERS} and inject extra sections. Migration: update both to read lavra-worker/SKILL.md via cat instead. One-line change each. No internal phase references — clean dependency on template only.

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: DECISION: subagent prompt template goes in lavra-work-multi/references/subagent-prompt.md not as a standalone skill — it is an asset of the multi-bead orchestrator, not independently invocable

comments.author: Roberto Mello
comments.text: PATTERN: skills/X/references/ directory holds assets read via cat by skill X — not invocable, no frontmatter, clearly scoped to owner

title: Cursor installer: add skills support
